commit.message:
Fix strict eval function declaration leak to global scope

Strict-mode eval (both direct and indirect) should scope function
declarations locally — they must not leak to the caller's global
environment. The eval VM shared the same global_this GC pointer as
the caller, so DefineGlobal during eval execution wrote directly to
the shared object. The writeback loop correctly skipped copying to
self.globals, but global_this was already mutated.

Fix: when the writeback loop skips a strict-scoped declaration, also
remove it from global_this to undo the eval VM's direct write.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix WeakMap/WeakSet constructors and cross-realm BigInt JSON panic

- WeakMap/WeakSet: route NewCall through call_builtin (like Map/Set) so
  constructor creates VmObject wrapper with __map_data__/__set_data__,
  __type__, and correct prototype chain. Previous code pushed bare VmMap/
  VmSet values without prototype, causing method calls to fail.

- Add BUILTIN_CTOR_WEAKMAP and BUILTIN_CTOR_WEAKSET handlers in
  call_builtin with full iterable consumption support.

- Fix cross-realm BigInt JSON.stringify panic: guard register_cross_realm_fn
  against remapping parent-realm functions stored on child-realm objects
  (ip >= child.chunk.code.len() check).

- Fix value display: add WeakMap/WeakSet/Map/Set type detection in both
  value_to_string and value_to_compact_result_string.

- Restore simple_object_method_source helper in compiler.

fix(pihole): remove drop:ALL, keep default root capabilities

Pi-hole FTL needs DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, CHOWN, SETGID, SETUID and
more to manage its log files and pid files. The network namespace
enforces privileged PSA so keeping default root capabilities is
safe and avoids capability whack-a-mole.

keeps FTL running as root and bypasses the capability elevation.

commit.message:
Fix async/await regression: route async.* host functions through call_host_fn

The call/apply/bind validation commit changed vm_call_function_value's
VmObject branch from call_host_fn to call_named_host_function_with_this.
However, call_named_host_function_with_this only recognizes specific
prefixes (promise., array., etc.) and falls through to
call_named_host_function for unrecognized names. The 'async.resume.fulfill'
and 'async.resume.reject' callbacks (used for await suspension/resumption)
start with 'async.' which was not in the prefix list, so they silently
dropped — causing await to never resume.

Add 'async.' to the prefix routing in call_named_host_function_with_this.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
